creator: jkois@freebsd.org
text:
Hello,

Thank you for your submission.  Please note that the page you are
referring to is NOT part of the official FreeBSD website and/or
documentation.  All sites under http://people.freebsd.org/ are PRIVATE
pages of FreeBSD developers and it is up to them what they store/publish
there.  So unless the developer fixes the page, there is nothing we can
do.  If the developers is not active anymore the site can still be
there.  But as already mentioned there is nothing we could do except
delete the whole user profile.  But we normally do not do that.

Regarding your question.  The real (and supported/up-to-date) page can
be found on the official FreeBSD website:

http://www.freebsd.org/advocacy/

Conclusion:  I will close this PR as the mentioned page is not part of
the official FreeBSD documentation.


Regards.

jkois

-- 
 Johann Kois
 jkois(at)FreeBSD.org
 FreeBSD Documentation Project
 FreeBSD German Documentation Project - https://doc.bsdgroup.de
